raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a formal complaint against Citibank for a clear violation of the XXXX XXXX Billing Act ( FCBA ) regarding " services not rendered. '' On XX/XX/year>, my flight ( Wizz Air # XXXX from XXXX XXXX to XXXX ) was completely cancelled by the airline. Please note that I live in the XXXX and purchased these airline tickets in the XXXX. 

Because the airline cancelled the flight, they failed to provide the service I purchased. Under XXXX regulations and basic contract law, I am legally entitled to a full cash refund. 
I initiated a credit card dispute with Citi for the amount of USD {$480.00} ( Case # XXXX ). However, Citi improperly sided with the merchant and reversed the dispute. The merchant ( XXXX XXXX ) misled Citi by XXXX the refund process was handled. In reality, the airline directed me to use their app to process the refund, promised app " credits '' within XXXX hours that could be converted to cash, and then completely failed to deliver those credits. I have received XXXX credits, XXXX funds, and no flight. 

I have exhausted all efforts to resolve this. I sent multiple emails to the merchant in XX/XX/year> which went entirely ignored. When XXXX XXXX finally replied on XX/XX/year>, they provided a broken, non-functional web form link, which is a bad-faith stalling tactic. 

I sent a comprehensive appeal letter via email to XXXX XXXX XXXX Department on XX/XX/year>, outlining this breakdown, providing proof of the airline 's cancellation, and showing the merchant 's failure to perform. Citi has ignored my communication ( no response to my email as of today, XX/XX/year> ) and is currently facilitating a merchants fraudulent retention of my funds for a service they never provided. 

Per the XXXX, I am not liable for charges where the merchant fails to deliver the promised services. I request that Citi immediately reopen this case and permanently credit my account for USD {$480.00}.
